date-idea: Museum is the move if its too hot outside and both of you are artistic or into fantasy/Sci-Fi
Farmers Market is a nice stroll, great ice breaker if one of you can lead/yap
Walk in the park or a dog park if you guys have pets. Nice if both of you are low maintenance
Picnic in the park. High effort but fun. Bring games, snacks, drinks, a blanket, and positivity
Zoo is always a good time with and a perfect ice breaker
Hiking but only if you're in shape
Thrifting is perfect if both of y'all are artsy
Escape Room is perfectfor the overachievers and overthinkers
Aquarium, just like museum but for people who are more into animals than art
Mini Golf for the competitive types
Arcade for the retro lovers but also for the alcoholics
Pickleball for people who already know how to play or actually want to learn
Billiards for the vibes, drinks, and music
Tennis...if both of you play sports
Fishing... but only if shes really into you and you know how to fish. Shes your number one fan and you like her company
Bouldering, for the adventure seekers and gym rats
Rollerblading/Ice Skating for the people who aren't scared to live freely
Driving Range(golf) is nice if you're both are new and want to learn or are experienced
Driving Range(golf) is nice if you're both are new and want to learn or are experienced
Shooting Range is a fun short exciting date, great ice breaker, and gets the adrenaline going
Bowling if you enjoy roasting and yapping but also for the vibes
Cooking Class for the people who are both really shy or socially awkward
Dancing Class if you both love music. Second date only since its intimate
Painting Class for the people who are both really shy or socially awkward
Ceramic Class for the people who are both really shy or socially awkward
Ikea Run to catch up but to also see if you can live with each other
Movies are perfect way to see how each other thinks and feels
Hobbies
Barnes & Noble is a great place to connect with each other if you both enjoy reading
Concerts for the music lovers and dancers. Pick a random artist/genre and just go in blind
Raves, only works if you both are into that kind of thing
Carnivals...require little planning but a lot of money. Only fun if there are rides
Lunch/Dinner for the yappers and foodies
Library is a perfect place if you both need to get work done or some light reading
Jigsaw Puzzles are a must. Throw a movie or music on in the background, get some snacks and vibe
Board Games are great if you're both comptetive or are still getting to know each other
Renaissance Faire is a such a unique experience, great for all types of people
Travel - road-trip, cruise, out-of-state, national parks, etc.
DIY Projects are a great way to stay creative and have fun together. Paint a room or build something
Anime Convention for the anime fans. Dress up even if you're not into anime. Like halloween but better
Amtrak Trip is a great way to see the country and spend time together. Low energy
